canonical: How do I register for courses and exams at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What are the steps to sign up for a course or an examination?
Where do I actually enrol in my lectures and exams once I'm admitted?
Can you walk me through the course and exam registration process?
If I want to book a place in a seminar and its exam, what do I do?
weird_framing: ok so i'm finally admitted, now like how do i even get into the classes... is it in that uspace thing or ufind or
key_fact: You register for courses and exams via u:space (you are forwarded there from u:find), and you need an active u:account plus a valid admission to a degree programme. As long as you register within the registration period, the exact moment you register does not matter — there is no 'first come, first served'; places are allocated only after the deadline.

canonical: How many ECTS credits do I need to earn in my first four semesters?
paraphrases: What is the minimum number of credits I must complete early in my bachelor's, and by when?
Is there a required amount of ECTS I have to reach or I lose my place?
How many credit points must a new bachelor student achieve within two years?
What's the minimum study output rule for the first four semesters?
weird_framing: heard there's some rule where if you don't rack up enough credits early they boot you... how many is it and how long do i have
key_fact: In every bachelor's and diploma programme (for students admitted since winter semester 2022/23) you must obtain at least 16 ECTS credits within the first four semesters. Otherwise your admission expires and you are excluded from that programme for four semesters.

canonical: What does it mean to be 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination-active)?
paraphrases: When is a student counted as examination-active, and does it matter for me personally?
Can you explain 'prüfungsaktiv' and whether there are consequences if I'm not?
How many credits per year make me 'examination-active', and why does the university care?
What's the difference between being examination-active and just studying normally?
weird_framing: my advisor mentioned pruefungsaktiv or whatever... do i get in trouble if i'm not that, or is it just a uni money thing?
key_fact: You count as 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination-active) if you complete at least 16 ECTS credits (or at least 8 SWS) in a degree programme within one academic year. It matters for the university's funding, not for the student personally — not being examination-active has no study-law consequences for you (unlike the separate minimum-number-of-credits rule).

canonical: When does course registration open for English and American Studies in summer semester 2026?
paraphrases: What are the registration phase dates for continuous-assessment courses in English and American Studies for 2026S?
For summer 2026, when can I sign up for pi-LV courses in the English and American Studies programme?
What's the registration window for English and American Studies seminars in the summer 2026 semester?
On which dates does the SPL English and American Studies open course registration for summer semester 2026?
weird_framing: anglistik people — when does the anmeldung open for the SS26 continuous-assessment courses again? like the exact start day/time
key_fact: For summer semester 2026, course registration for continuous-assessment (pi LV) courses in English and American Studies runs from 6 February 2026 at 12:00 to 20 February 2026 at 12:00. Each directorate of studies (SPL) has its own registration window, and you should always confirm individual course deadlines in u:find.

canonical: What happens if I miss the first session of a continuous-assessment course?
paraphrases: If I'm registered for a seminar (pi LV) but don't show up to the first unit, do I get removed?
Is attendance at the first meeting of a continuous-assessment course mandatory?
Can I lose my spot in an exercise course by skipping the opening session?
What are the attendance rules for courses with continuous assessment, especially the first class?
weird_framing: uhh i signed up for a seminar but might sleep through the first class... do they just yeet me off the list if i'm not there?
key_fact: Courses with continuous assessment (pi LV, e.g. seminars and exercises) have compulsory attendance; if you are registered and are absent from the first unit without a good reason, you are deregistered. Places are allocated by the point system, preference system or recommended path of study, and unused registration points cannot be carried over to the next semester.

canonical: When is the application deadline to apply for a degree programme for winter semester 2026/27?
paraphrases: What is the application period for the winter semester 2026/27, and does it differ by citizenship?
By when do EU and non-EU applicants have to apply for winter 2026/27?
If I miss the application deadline, is there any grace period?
What are the application dates for a bachelor or master starting in winter 2026/27?
weird_framing: trying to apply for this coming winter term... whats the last day, and is it earlier if you're from outside the EU?
key_fact: For winter semester 2026/27 the general application period for EU/EEA/CH citizens is 22 June to 5 September 2026; for third-country nationals it ends earlier, on 3 August 2026. There is no grace period — if you miss the application period you must wait for the next semester.

canonical: Which bachelor programmes at the University of Vienna are taught in English?
paraphrases: Are there any English-language bachelor's programmes here?
Can I study a bachelor programme in English at the University of Vienna?
Which undergraduate programmes are offered in English or bilingually?
What are the English-taught bachelor options at Uni Vienna?
weird_framing: my german is rough — is there literally any bachelor here i can do in english, or is it all german?
key_fact: The University of Vienna offers only two English-language/bilingual bachelor programmes: International Legal Studies (with an entrance exam procedure) and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (with an aptitude assessment procedure). All other bachelor/diploma programmes are taught in German.

canonical: I have a disability — can I take my exams in a different format?
paraphrases: Am I entitled to an alternative mode of assessment because of my impairment?
How do I arrange a modified exam format to compensate for a disability?
What is an alternative mode of assessment and how do I apply for one?
Can exams be adapted for students with impairments, and what's the procedure?
weird_framing: got adhd and standard exams wreck me — can they let me do it differently, and do i have to file something with the SPL or just ask the teacher?
key_fact: Students with a study-relevant impairment have a legal right to an alternative mode of assessment (same content and required performance, but a different/flexible mode). You arrange it either by directly agreeing with the lecturer/examiner or by formal application to the directorate of studies (SPL) with a specialist medical certificate. A granted application does not notify teachers automatically — you must inform each lecturer yourself.

canonical: How much is the tuition fee at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What do I actually pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees?
How much does it cost each semester for an EU student versus a non-EU student?
What are the fee amounts, and when does the tuition fee apply for EU citizens?
Do EU/EEA students pay tuition, or just the Students' Union fee?
weird_framing: asking for a friend — that uni vienna money thing per semester, ballpark? and do eu people even pay the big fee or just the small one
key_fact: EU/EEA/Switzerland citizens pay only the ~26.20 EUR Students' Union fee each semester during the stipulated programme duration plus two tolerance semesters; the ~363.36 EUR tuition fee (total ~389.56 EUR) applies only if they exceed that period. Non-EU/EEA citizens generally pay ~726.72 EUR tuition from the first semester, with some exemptions.

canonical: What is u:space and what can I do with it?
paraphrases: What is the u:space portal used for during my studies?
Which tasks can I handle in u:space — fees, registration, documents?
Can you explain the main functions of u:space?
How does u:space fit into organising my studies at the University of Vienna?
weird_framing: everyone keeps saying 'do it in uspace' — what even is that portal and what all can you actually do in it?
key_fact: u:space is the University of Vienna's portal for organising your studies, accessed with your u:account: you use it to apply for admission, order your u:card, pay the tuition/Students' Union fee, register for and deregister from courses and exams, check grades in your record of examinations, and download and print your study documents.
